sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
7 technology-scope features assessed against NAICS 333415. Kano classification + 9-dimension ODI fit scoring per feature. Top 10 market needs (from JTBD analysis) used as scoring context.

### Top 10 Market Needs (scoring context)

| Rank | ID | Statement (abbreviated) | Opp | Imp | Sat |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ES-07 | Detect empty-pipe / air-lock before compressor enable (<5s) | 14 | 9 | 4 |
| 2 | ES-16 | Prevent compressor short-cycling from undetected low-flow (>10s) | 13 | 9 | 5 |
| 3 | ES-06 | Auto-compensate for glycol concentration variations (<50%) | 12 | 8 | 4 |
| 4 | ES-09 | Maintain ±3% accuracy over 10,000 operating hours | 12 | 9 | 6 |
| 5 | ES-10 | Prevent air entrainment >2% vol causing signal loss | 12 | 8 | 4 |
| 6 | ES-25 | Demonstrate EU EcoDesign compliance via traceable flow data | 12 | 8 | 4 |
| 7 | ES-04 | Minimize incorrect orientation causing bias >2% | 11 | 8 | 5 |
| 8 | ES-11 | Measure accurately across full turndown range | 11 | 8 | 5 |
| 9 | ES-17 | Detect calibration drift >±0.5% within 30 days | 11 | 8 | 5 |
| 10 | ES-21 | Minimize acoustic coupling degradation causing >1% error in 5yr | 11 | 8 | 5 |

[SRC: JTBD_air_conditioning_and_warm_air_heating_eq]

### 09a — Per-Market Kano + ODI Fit Scoring

| Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Rel. | Skill | Stress | Pain | Conf. | Delight | Overall |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Transit-Time Flow Measurement | must_be | 5 | 4 | 7 | 8 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 7 | 2 | **5.00** |
| Integrated Temperature Sensing | attractive | 6 | 7 | 3 | 6 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 7 | 5 | **5.67** |
| Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path | one_dimensional | 2 | 7 | 2 | 3 | 2 | 2 | 3 | 2 | 3 | **2.89** |
| No Moving Parts | attractive | 3 | 8 | 3 | 9 | 6 | 6 | 8 | 6 | 5 | **6.00** |
| Bidirectional Flow Detection | attractive | 4 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 6 | 4 | **5.00** |
| Digital Anomaly Detection | attractive | 7 | 6 | 8 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 8 | 6 | **6.67** |
| Real-Time Compensation Algorithms | one_dimensional | 3 | 4 | 3 | 7 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 7 | 3 | **4.56** |

### 09b — Rationale per Feature

**Transit-Time Flow Measurement** (must_be, 5.00): Delivers the commodity FP — "quantify volumetric flow rate." Every HVAC system with hydronic circuits requires flow measurement; its absence is not tolerated. Primary drivers: Reliability (8) — no mechanical failure modes in the measurement principle; Safety (7) — enables compressor protection against low-flow and empty-pipe conditions (ES-16, ES-07). Market evidence: 35% installed base already uses ultrasonic transit-time [SRC: JTBD].

**Integrated Temperature Sensing** (attractive, 5.67): Not standard across all flowmeter technologies — separate temperature probes are the norm. Enables single-body kW calculation (flow x ΔT x Cp), directly addressing ES-20 (Opp 12). Primary drivers: Cost (7) — eliminates separate probe purchase, wiring, and commissioning; Pain (7) — removes the task of sourcing and mounting a second sensor; Confidence (7) — co-located measurement avoids spatial/temporal mismatch in energy calculation. Attractive because absence does not cause dissatisfaction (separate probes work), but presence creates disproportionate satisfaction.

**Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path** (one_dimensional, 2.89): More is proportionally better — every mbar saved reduces pump energy. Turbine meters impose 100–300 mbar; ultrasonic delivers <50 mbar (ES-15). Primary driver: Cost (7) — reduced pump sizing and continuous energy savings. All other dimensions are 1–3 because the benefit is narrowly hydraulic. One-dimensional because satisfaction scales linearly with pressure-drop reduction.

**No Moving Parts** (attractive, 6.00): Eliminates the primary failure mode of 30% of the installed base (turbine/impeller bearing wear). Not expected by buyers accustomed to mechanical sensors. Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — no bearing wear, no impeller jamming, no seal degradation; Cost (8) and Pain (8) — eliminates scheduled bearing replacement and unplanned downtime. Addresses ES-23 (minimize field recalibration) and ES-27 (minimize warranty callbacks). Attractive because turbine sensors are accepted as normal — zero maintenance is a genuine surprise.

**Bidirectional Flow Detection** (attractive, 5.00): Inherent to transit-time principle at no added cost. Detects check valve failure and pump malfunction (ES-14, Opp 10). Primary drivers: Safety (7) — catches potentially damaging reverse flow within 1 second; Confidence (6) — directional awareness builds system control trust. Attractive because paddle switches and basic turbine meters lack this capability entirely.

**Digital Anomaly Detection** (attractive, 6.67): Highest-scoring feature. Directly addresses the market's top underserved need ES-07 (Opp 14) — empty-pipe detection before compressor enable. Also addresses ES-10 (air entrainment signal loss, Opp 12). Primary drivers: Safety (8) — empty-pipe compressor start causes high-pressure trip or freeze damage; Confidence (8) — diagnostic flags validate measurement integrity in real time; Time (7) — immediate detection (<5s) vs waiting for compressor fault trip. Most flowmeters provide only the measurement itself — system health diagnostics are a genuine delighter.

**Real-Time Compensation Algorithms** (one_dimensional, 4.56): More accurate compensation = proportionally better measurement. Addresses ES-06 (glycol compensation, Opp 12) and ES-13 (temperature transient response). Primary drivers: Reliability (7) — maintains specified accuracy across operating envelope; Confidence (7) — knowing readings are compensated builds trust. One-dimensional because all modern sensors offer some compensation; better algorithms yield better accuracy linearly.

### 09b — Validation Summary

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Kano cross-market consistency | PASS — single market, no cross-market comparison needed |
| 2 | safety ≥ 7 but confidence ≤ 3 | PASS — no occurrences |
| 3 | skill ≥ 7 but stress ≤ 3 | PASS — no occurrences |
| 4 | overall_fit arithmetic | PASS — all 7 recomputed deterministically |
| 5 | No feature scores 10 on all dimensions | PASS — max single score is 9 (Reliability on No Moving Parts) |
| 6 | Feature names comply with 2–6 word noun phrases | PASS |
| 7 | Measurement accuracy feature scores 1 on Reliability | PASS — Transit-Time=8, Compensation=7 |

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs derived from the product's 8 constraints. Each need WOULD DISAPPEAR if a fundamentally different flow measurement technology were chosen (electromagnetic, Coriolis, turbine). Litmus test applied per need.

### 10a — Product-Related Needs

| # | Statement | Source Constraint | Imp | Sat | Litmus |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-01 | Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure from acoustic impedance mismatch when non-aqueous contaminants enter the hydronic circuit | Media Limitation | 4 | 6 | Electromagnetic/Coriolis unaffected by acoustic coupling — PASS |
| PRN-02 | Minimize the risk of polymer housing and seal degradation from sustained operation near the 90°C thermal limit in high-temperature heating configurations | Maximum Medium Temperature | 5 | 5 | Metal-bodied sensors (electromagnetic, Coriolis) tolerate >150°C — PASS |
| PRN-03 | Minimize the risk of sensor housing failure from pressure spikes exceeding 10 bar continuous rating during water hammer or pump surge events | Maximum Operating Pressure | 5 | 6 | Metal-bodied sensors tolerate >40 bar continuous — PASS |
| PRN-04 | Minimize the likelihood of measurement signal loss when system flow rate falls below the ultrasonic transit-time noise floor during part-load VFD operation | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | 7 | 5 | Coriolis meters measure to near-zero flow; ultrasonic has specific SNR threshold — PASS |
| PRN-05 | Minimize the likelihood of ultrasonic signal attenuation from air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume during thermal cycling or system startup | Air Bubble Tolerance | 8 | 4 | Electromagnetic meters are insensitive to air bubbles; turbine meters less affected — PASS |
| PRN-06 | Minimize the likelihood of complete measurement loss from partially filled pipe sections during system charging, maintenance drain-down, or gravity-return operation | Pipe Full Condition | 6 | 5 | DP sensors function in partially filled pipes; electromagnetic recovers faster — PASS |
| PRN-07 | Minimize the sensitivity of transit-time measurement accuracy to asymmetric flow profiles from elbows, valves, or tees in compact equipment enclosures | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | 7 | 4 | Coriolis sensors are orientation/profile-independent; ultrasonic requires laminar profile — PASS |
| PRN-08 | Minimize the likelihood of transit-time measurement error from rapid speed-of-sound changes during heating-cooling mode transitions exceeding compensation algorithm response time | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | 7 | 5 | Electromagnetic/Coriolis measurement insensitive to speed of sound — PASS |

### 10b — Validation Summary

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Litmus test — would need disappear with different technology? | PASS — all 8 needs are specific to ultrasonic transit-time principle |
| 2 | Source constraint traceability — constraint exists? | PASS — all 8 constraints from 01_product_decomposition |
| 3 | Affected market plausibility | PASS — all 8 constraints manifest in HVAC hydronic circuits |
| 4 | Completeness — every constraint generates ≥1 need | PASS — 8 constraints → 8 needs (1:1) |
| 5 | No market-level needs included | PASS — none duplicate Step 06 error statements |
| 6 | importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7 | PASS — no occurrences |
| 7 | Error-first statement format | PASS — all "Minimize the likelihood of…" or "Minimize the risk of…" |

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature-Market Fit (Marquardt)
sections.content:
6 vendor-scope features assessed against NAICS 333415. Vendor implementation scored against best incumbent on 0–10 scale. Advantage: superior (>1pt gap), parity (≤1pt), inferior (>1pt behind).

### 11a — Vendor vs. Incumbent Comparison

| Feature | Kano | Vendor | Incumbent | Inc. Tech | Adv. |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Modular Pipe Diameter Variants | one_dimensional | 7 | 5 | Turbine / Impeller Sensor | **superior** |
| Customizable Communication Interface | one_dimensional | 7 | 6 | Ultrasonic (Kamstrup) | parity |
| Customizable Mechanical Interface | one_dimensional | 6 | 6 | Turbine / Impeller Sensor | parity |
| Low-Energy Battery Mode | indifferent | 8 | 2 | Ultrasonic (generic) | superior* |
| Pre-Calibrated Delivery | must_be | 8 | 7 | Ultrasonic (Kamstrup/Danfoss) | parity |
| KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval | must_be | 7 | 8 | Ultrasonic (Kamstrup) | parity |

*Superior implementation but indifferent Kano class — market does not value battery mode in OEM HVAC equipment.

### 11a — Rationale per Feature

**Modular Pipe Diameter Variants** (one_dimensional, superior): Marquardt offers DN12/14/20/25 sharing one electronics platform and RAST 2.5 connector. OEMs produce multiple capacity variants — shared electronics across DN sizes reduces BOM complexity and qualification effort (ES-02). Best incumbent turbine sensors typically require separate electronics per DN size. Vendor 7 vs incumbent 5 → gap of 2 = superior.

**Customizable Communication Interface** (one_dimensional, parity): Marquardt offers UART, LIN, and frequency output selectable at order level. Competing ultrasonic sensors (Kamstrup, Danfoss) offer 2–3 protocol options with similar flexibility. Vendor 7 vs incumbent 6 → gap of 1 = parity.

**Customizable Mechanical Interface** (one_dimensional, parity): Marquardt offers threaded and plug-in pipe connections. Competing sensors also offer adapter systems for OEM integration. Both meet OEM assembly line requirements. Vendor 6 vs incumbent 6 → gap of 0 = parity.

**Low-Energy Battery Mode** (indifferent, superior*): Marquardt achieves genuine <5 μA standby current enabling battery-powered operation. Most HVAC inline sensors require mains power. However, in NAICS 333415, all OEM equipment has permanent DC power supply — battery mode is unused. Vendor 8 vs incumbent 2 → gap of 6, but Kano = indifferent neutralizes the advantage.

**Pre-Calibrated Delivery** (must_be, parity): Marquardt delivers individually calibrated to customer-specific medium with 100% automated final test. Kamstrup and Danfoss offer factory calibration with standard media profiles. OEMs expect calibrated delivery — this is table stakes. Vendor 8 vs incumbent 7 → gap of 1 = parity.

**KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval** (must_be, parity): Marquardt holds KTW (German) and WRAS (UK) approvals. Kamstrup holds KTW, WRAS, and NSF/ANSI 61 (US). For HVAC hydronic circuits the KTW/WRAS coverage is sufficient, but Kamstrup's broader set gives slight edge. Vendor 7 vs incumbent 8 → gap of 1 = parity.

### 11b — Bias Check

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | >70% superior verdicts (vendor bias) | PASS — 1 of 6 = 17% (2 if counting indifferent) |
| 2 | >70% inferior verdicts (anti-vendor bias) | PASS — 0 of 6 = 0% |
| 3 | Advantage classification arithmetic | PASS — all 6 entries verified |
| 4 | must_be features: both scores ≥ 5 | PASS — Pre-Calibrated (8/7), KTW/WRAS (7/8) |
| 5 | Rationale references specific specs | PASS — DN sizes, protocols, μA figures cited |
| 6 | No straw-man incumbents | PASS — Kamstrup/Danfoss are genuine market leaders |

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
### Feature Fit Ranking (Step 09)

| Rank | Feature | Kano | Overall Fit | Primary Value |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Digital Anomaly Detection | attractive | **6.67** | Empty-pipe/air-lock detection (ES-07, Opp 14) |
| 2 | No Moving Parts | attractive | **6.00** | Zero mechanical maintenance (ES-23, ES-27) |
| 3 | Integrated Temperature Sensing | attractive | **5.67** | Single-body kW calculation (ES-20, Opp 12) |
| 4 | Transit-Time Flow Measurement | must_be | **5.00** | Core commodity FP delivery |
| 5 | Bidirectional Flow Detection | attractive | **5.00** | Reverse flow / check valve fault (ES-14) |
| 6 | Real-Time Compensation Algorithms | one_dimensional | **4.56** | Glycol + temperature compensation (ES-06, ES-13) |
| 7 | Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path | one_dimensional | **2.89** | Pump energy savings (ES-15) |

### Vendor Competitive Position (Step 11)

| Verdict | Count | Features |
|---|---|---|
| Superior | 1 | Modular Pipe Diameter Variants |
| Parity | 4 | Communication Interface, Mechanical Interface, Pre-Calibrated Delivery, KTW/WRAS |
| Inferior | 0 | — |
| Superior (indifferent market) | 1 | Low-Energy Battery Mode |

**Key insight:** Marquardt's strongest competitive advantage in NAICS 333415 is the modular DN platform (4 variants, shared electronics), which directly addresses OEM multi-capacity product line needs. Most other vendor features are at parity with established incumbents (Kamstrup, Danfoss). The technology-scope delighters — Digital Anomaly Detection, No Moving Parts, Integrated Temperature — provide the strongest market pull, but these are shared with all ultrasonic transit-time sensors, not Marquardt-specific.

### Product-Related Risk Summary (Step 10)

| Risk Level | Constraint | PRN | Imp | Sat |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **High** | Air Bubble Tolerance | PRN-05 | 8 | 4 |
| **Medium** | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | PRN-07 | 7 | 4 |
| **Medium** | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | PRN-04 | 7 | 5 |
| **Medium** | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | PRN-08 | 7 | 5 |
| **Medium** | Pipe Full Condition | PRN-06 | 6 | 5 |
| **Low** | Maximum Medium Temperature | PRN-02 | 5 | 5 |
| **Low** | Maximum Operating Pressure | PRN-03 | 5 | 6 |
| **Low** | Media Limitation | PRN-01 | 4 | 6 |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Step 09: all 7 technology-scope features assessed | PASS |
| 2 | Step 09: Kano class + 9 ODI scores + overall_fit per feature | PASS |
| 3 | Step 09: overall_fit = arithmetic mean (deterministic) | PASS |
| 4 | Step 09: rationale references market needs with IDs | PASS |
| 5 | Step 10: all 8 constraints generate ≥1 product-related need | PASS |
| 6 | Step 10: litmus test applied to every need | PASS |
| 7 | Step 10: no market-level needs duplicated from Step 06 | PASS |
| 8 | Step 10: error-first statement format | PASS |
| 9 | Step 11: all 6 vendor-scope features assessed | PASS |
| 10 | Step 11: vendor vs best incumbent scoring with advantage | PASS |
| 11 | Step 11: bias check passed (mix of verdicts) | PASS |
| 12 | Feature names: 2–6 word noun phrases, no brands/superlatives | PASS |
| 13 | No web search used — all derived from upstream files | PASS |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [SRC: 01_product_decomposition] — Technology-scope features, vendor-scope features, constraints, UNSPSC classification
- [SRC: 02_functional_promise] — Commodity FP, product FP, FP extension, complements
- [SRC: 05_market_discovery] — NAICS 333415 excluded as home market; architecture distance scoring criteria
- [SRC: JTBD_air_conditioning_and_warm_air_heating_eq] — 27 error statements, alternatives, scenarios, job map

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Detect empty-pipe / air-lock before compressor enable (<5s)
Prevent compressor short-cycling from undetected low-flow (>10s)
Prevent air entrainment >2% vol causing signal loss
Demonstrate EU EcoDesign compliance via traceable flow data
Measure accurately across full turndown range
Minimize acoustic coupling degradation causing >1% error in 5yr

rows.Result: PASS — single market, no cross-market comparison needed

rows.Check: No feature scores 10 on all dimensions
rows.Result: PASS — max single score is 9 (Reliability on No Moving Parts)

rows.Check: Feature names comply with 2–6 word noun phrases
Measurement accuracy feature scores 1 on Reliability

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure from acoustic impedance mismatch when non-aqueous contaminants enter the hydronic circuit
rows.Litmus: Electromagnetic/Coriolis unaffected by acoustic coupling — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the risk of polymer housing and seal degradation from sustained operation near the 90°C thermal limit in high-temperature heating configurations
Minimize the risk of sensor housing failure from pressure spikes exceeding 10 bar continuous rating during water hammer or pump surge events
rows.Litmus: Metal-bodied sensors tolerate >40 bar continuous — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of measurement signal loss when system flow rate falls below the ultrasonic transit-time noise floor during part-load VFD operation
rows.Litmus: Coriolis meters measure to near-zero flow; ultrasonic has specific SNR threshold — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of ultrasonic signal attenuation from air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume during thermal cycling or system startup
rows.Litmus: Electromagnetic meters are insensitive to air bubbles; turbine meters less affected — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of complete measurement loss from partially filled pipe sections during system charging, maintenance drain-down, or gravity-return operation
rows.Litmus: DP sensors function in partially filled pipes; electromagnetic recovers faster — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the sensitivity of transit-time measurement accuracy to asymmetric flow profiles from elbows, valves, or tees in compact equipment enclosures
rows.Litmus: Coriolis sensors are orientation/profile-independent; ultrasonic requires laminar profile — PASS

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of transit-time measurement error from rapid speed-of-sound changes during heating-cooling mode transitions exceeding compensation algorithm response time
rows.Litmus: Electromagnetic/Coriolis measurement insensitive to speed of sound — PASS

rows.Check: Litmus test — would need disappear with different technology?
rows.Result: PASS — all 8 needs are specific to ultrasonic transit-time principle
PASS — all 8 constraints manifest in HVAC hydronic circuits
PASS — none duplicate Step 06 error statements
PASS — all "Minimize the likelihood of…" or "Minimize the risk of…"
PASS — DN sizes, protocols, μA figures cited
PASS — Kamstrup/Danfoss are genuine market leaders

rows.Features: Communication Interface, Mechanical Interface, Pre-Calibrated Delivery, KTW/WRAS

rows.Check: Step 09: Kano class + 9 ODI scores + overall_fit per feature
Step 09: rationale references market needs with IDs
Step 10: all 8 constraints generate ≥1 product-related need
Step 10: litmus test applied to every need
Step 10: no market-level needs duplicated from Step 06
Step 11: vendor vs best incumbent scoring with advantage
Step 11: bias check passed (mix of verdicts)
Feature names: 2–6 word noun phrases, no brands/superlatives
No web search used — all derived from upstream files

entities.naics_title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
entities.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entities.commodity_functional_promise: Quantify the volumetric or mass flow rate of fluids traversing a closed conduit

step_09_tech_feature_market_fit.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Delivers commodity FP — flow measurement is table stakes in hydronic HVAC. Primary drivers: Reliability (8) — no mechanical failure modes; Safety (7) — enables compressor protection (ES-16 Opp 13, ES-07 Opp 14). 35% installed base already uses ultrasonic.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Not standard across flowmeter technologies — separate probes are the norm. Enables single-body kW calculation (ES-20 Opp 12). Primary drivers: Cost (7) — eliminates separate probe; Pain (7) — removes second sensor sourcing/wiring; Confidence (7) — co-located measurement avoids spatial mismatch.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Benefit is narrowly hydraulic — turbine meters impose 100-300 mbar vs ultrasonic <50 mbar (ES-15 Opp 8). Primary driver: Cost (7) — reduced pump sizing and continuous energy savings over equipment lifetime. All other dimensions 1-3 because the benefit doesn't extend beyond hydraulic efficiency.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Eliminates the primary failure mode of 30% installed base (turbine bearing wear). Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — no bearing/impeller failure modes; Cost (8) and Pain (8) — eliminates scheduled bearing replacement and unplanned downtime. Addresses ES-23 (minimize recalibration) and ES-27 (minimize warranty callbacks). Attractive because mechanical sensors are accepted as normal.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Inherent to transit-time principle at no added cost. Detects check valve failure and pump malfunction (ES-14 Opp 10). Primary driver: Safety (7) — catches reverse flow within 1 second. Attractive because paddle switches and basic turbine meters lack directional awareness entirely.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Highest-scoring feature. Directly addresses top underserved need ES-07 (Opp 14) — empty-pipe detection <5s before compressor enable. Also addresses ES-10 (air entrainment signal loss, Opp 12). Primary drivers: Safety (8) — prevents compressor damage from dry start; Confidence (8) — diagnostic flags validate measurement in real time; Time (7) — immediate detection vs waiting for compressor fault.

entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Better compensation = better measurement, linearly. Addresses ES-06 (glycol compensation, Opp 12) and ES-13 (temperature transients). Primary drivers: Reliability (7) — maintains accuracy across operating envelope; Confidence (7) — compensated readings build trust. One-dimensional because all modern sensors offer some compensation.

step_10_product_related_needs.underlying_mechanism: Piezoelectric transducers in transit-time configuration; transit-time difference proportional to fluid velocity

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment

entries.feature_description: Serve multiple pipe sizes via interchangeable flow bodies sharing one electronics platform and RAST 2.5 connector
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt DN12/14/20/25 share one electronics platform and RAST 2.5 connector. Incumbent turbine sensors typically require separate electronics per DN size, increasing OEM BOM complexity. Gap of 2 points = superior. Directly addresses ES-02 (multi-DN platform without hydraulic redesign).

entries.feature_description: Support UART, LIN, and frequency output protocols selectable at order level for diverse controller architectures
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt offers 3 protocols (UART, LIN, frequency). Kamstrup/Danfoss offer 2-3 protocol options with similar flexibility. Gap of 1 point = parity. LIN automotive-grade bus is a minor differentiator for heat pump OEMs with automotive supply chain origins.

entries.feature_description: Accept threaded or plug-in pipe connections to match OEM assembly line requirements
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt offers threaded and plug-in options. Incumbent turbine and ultrasonic sensors also offer adapter systems for OEM integration. Gap of 0 = parity. Both meet OEM assembly line requirements for <3 min integration (ES-03).

entries.feature_description: Operate at ultra-low current consumption (<5 μA standby) for battery-powered remote installations
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt achieves genuine <5 μA standby, enabling real battery operation. Most HVAC inline sensors require permanent mains/DC power. However, in OEM HVAC equipment (heat pumps, chillers), all units have permanent DC supply — battery mode is unused. Kano = indifferent neutralizes the 6-point gap.

entries.feature_description: Ship fully calibrated and tested to customer-specific medium and flow profile with 100% automated final test
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt calibrates to customer-specific medium (e.g., 30% glycol mix) with 100% automated test. Kamstrup/Danfoss offer factory calibration with standard media profiles. Gap of 1 = parity. OEMs expect calibrated delivery — this is table stakes (must_be). Marquardt's customer-specific calibration is a minor edge.

entries.feature_description: Wetted parts approved for potable water contact under KTW (German) and WRAS (UK) standards
entries.market_name: Air Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment
entries.rationale: Marquardt holds KTW and WRAS — the two major European standards. Kamstrup holds KTW, WRAS, plus NSF/ANSI 61 (US market access). For European HVAC hydronic circuits, KTW/WRAS is sufficient. Gap of 1 = parity, though Kamstrup has broader geographic coverage.
